draft.introduction: Yoga Sutra Book 2 (Sadhana Pada) presents the practical path of yoga, including the eight limbs (ashtanga), the five afflictions (kleshas), and the concept of karma. It is the systematic manual of yogic practice.

translation.text: Austerity, self-study, and surrender to God constitute the yoga of action (kriya yoga). Its purpose is to cultivate samadhi and to attenuate the afflictions. The eight limbs of yoga are: restraint, observance, posture, breath control, withdrawal of the senses, concentration, meditation, and absorption.

translator_notes.note: The eight limbs (ashtanga) move systematically from external ethics (yama/niyama) through body (asana/pranayama) to mind (pratyahara/dharana/dhyana) to transcendence (samadhi). Modern yoga typically emphasizes only the third limb (posture), which Patanjali treats as preliminary.
